title: OpenShift DeploymentConfig Rollout Failures and Trigger-Driven ReplicationController Lifecycle
exclusions: Does not replace UC-3.2.6 Kubernetes Deployment and ReplicaSet rollout analytics on apps/v1, UC-3.3.7 BuildConfig and Build failure detection on build.openshift.io, UC-3.3.1 ClusterVersion upgrade progression, UC-3.2.15 DaemonSet rollout drift, or UC-3.3.17 MachineConfigPool degradation. Does not by itself remediate rollouts; it produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved OpenShift CLI and GitOps actions.
evidence: Saved search openshift_uc_3_3_16_deploymentconfig_rollout with fifteen minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns on ocp_deploymentconfig ocp_replicationcontroller ocp_imagestreamtag k8s_events ocp_audit and prometheus replicationcontroller lines; alert stanza with platform and application routing keys; weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab OpenShift project, configure a DeploymentConfig with a failing lifecycle post hook, run oc rollout latest under change control, wait until oc describe shows Progressing with ProgressDeadlineExceeded or ReplicaFailure and events cite hook failure, ingest ocp_deploymentconfig k8s_events and hook-lane events, execute openshift_uc_3_3_16_deploymentconfig_rollout, and expect page or warn with non-null recommended_action within fifteen minutes.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Restore a healthy hook command, complete the rollout until Available is true and Progressing is false, confirm oc get rc shows a single primary RC generation serving ready replicas, and verify the alert wrapper filtered to page and warn emits zero qualifying rows for that dc_name across thirty minutes.

dataSources: index=ocp_events or index=ocp_metrics with sourcetype=ocp_deploymentconfig for periodic oc get deploymentconfig -o json snapshots flattening metadata.namespace, metadata.name, status.latestVersion, status.availableReplicas, status.replicas, status.unavailableReplicas, status.conditions types Available Progressing ReplicaFailure with status reason and message including Progressing.reason tokens NewReplicationControllerCreated NewReplicationControllerAvailable ProgressDeadlineExceeded ReplicationControllerUpdated, spec.strategy.type Rolling Recreate Custom with nested rollingParams maxSurge maxUnavailable timeoutSeconds intervalSeconds updatePeriodSeconds and recreateParams timeoutSeconds; index=ocp_events sourcetype=ocp_replicationcontroller for ReplicationController objects owned by DeploymentConfig with status.replicas status.readyReplicas metadata.labels openshift.io/deployment-config.name and metadata.annotations openshift.io/deployment.phase when present; index=ocp_events sourcetype=ocp_imagestreamtag for imagestream.image.openshift.io/v1 ImageStreamTag metadata.name generation and tag history bumps correlating to ImageChange triggers; index=ocp_events sourcetype=k8s_events for per-namespace Kubernetes Events where involvedObject.kind is DeploymentConfig or ReplicationController with reason DeploymentCreated DeploymentScaled DeploymentRolledBack DeploymentPaused DeploymentResumed ProgressDeadlineExceeded FailedRollout and OpenShift rollout controller messages; index=ocp_audit sourcetype=ocp_audit for audit records on apps.openshift.io deploymentconfigs and replicationcontrollers patch create update; optional index=ocp_metrics sourcetype=prometheus:metrics for kube-state-metrics kube_replicationcontroller_status_ready_replicas kube_replicationcontroller_spec_replicas when exposed.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.3.16 OpenShift DeploymentConfig rollout reliability — ocp_deploymentconfig ocp_replicationcontroller ocp_imagestreamtag k8s_events prometheus RC metrics. Tunables: earliest=-6h@h latest=now; stuck_prog_min=25 overlap_warn=2")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=ocp_events OR index=ocp_audit) sourcetype="ocp_deploymentconfig" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, metadata_namespace, object_namespace, ""))))
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dc_name, name, metadata_name, deploymentconfig_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval latest_version=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(latest_version, status_latestVersion, dc_latest_version, "0")),10)
      | eval avail_rep=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(avail_rep, availableReplicas, status_availableReplicas, "0")),10)
      | eval spec_rep=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(spec_rep, replicas, status_replicas, spec_replicas, "0")),10)
      | eval av_ok=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(conditions_available_status, available_status, cond_available, ""))))
      | eval pr_ok=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(conditions_progressing_status, progressing_status, cond_progressing, ""))))
      | eval rf_ok=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(conditions_replicafailure_status, replicafailure_status, cond_replicafailure, ""))))
      | eval progressing_reason=trim(toString(coalesce(conditions_progressing_reason, progressing_reason, prog_reason, "")))
      | eval strategy_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(strategy_type, spec_strategy_type, strategy, ""))))
      | eval avail_flag=if(match(av_ok,"false|0"),1,0)
      | eval prog_flag=if(match(pr_ok,"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval repfail_flag=if(match(rf_ok,"true|1"),1,0)
      | sort 0 + cluster + namespace + dc_name - _time
      | streamstats window=3 current=t global=f last(latest_version) AS prev_ver last(avail_rep) AS prev_avail BY cluster namespace dc_name
      | eval ver_delta=latest_version-coalesce(prev_ver,latest_version)
      | eval avail_delta=avail_rep-coalesce(prev_avail,avail_rep)
      | eval stuck_version_creep=if(ver_delta>0 AND avail_delta<=0 AND prog_flag==1,1,0)
      | eval lane="dc_lane"
      | fields _time cluster namespace dc_name latest_version avail_rep spec_rep avail_flag prog_flag repfail_flag progressing_reason strategy_type stuck_version_creep lane ]
    [ search index=ocp_events sourcetype="ocp_replicationcontroller" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, metadata_namespace, ""))))
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dc_name, label_openshift_io_deployment_config_name, openshift_io_deployment_config_name, deployment_config_name, ""))))
      | eval rc_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(rc_name, name, metadata_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval rc_ready=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rc_ready_rep, readyReplicas, status_readyReplicas, "0")),10)
      | eval rc_spec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rc_spec_rep, replicas, status_replicas, "0")),10)
      | eval lane="rc_lane"
      | where len(dc_name)>0
      | eval rc_active=if(rc_ready>0 OR rc_spec>0,1,0)
      | stats dc(rc_name) AS rc_distinct sum(rc_active) AS rc_active_rows sum(eval(if(rc_ready>0,1,0))) AS rc_ready_pos count AS rc_obs BY cluster namespace dc_name lane ]
    [ search index=ocp_events sourcetype="ocp_imagestreamtag" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, metadata_namespace, ""))))
      | eval ist_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(ist_name, name, metadata_name, tag_name, ""))))
      | eval ist_gen=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ist_gen, generation, tag_generation, "0")),10)
      | eval lane="ist_lane"
      | sort 0 + cluster + namespace + ist_name - _time
      | streamstats window=2 current=t global=f last(ist_gen) AS prev_igen BY cluster namespace ist_name
      | eval ist_bump=if(ist_gen>coalesce(prev_igen,ist_gen),1,0)
      | stats max(_time) AS last_ist_t max(ist_bump) AS ist_bump_any BY cluster namespace
      | eval lane="ist_lane" ]
    [ search index=ocp_events (sourcetype="k8s_events" OR sourcetype="ocp_events") earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval kind=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_kind, involved_kind, object_kind, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_namespace, involved_namespace, object_namespace, namespace, ""))))
      | eval obj=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name, involved_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval reason_ev=trim(toString(coalesce(reason, Reason, event_reason, "")))
      | eval msg_ev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(message, Message, event_message, ""))))
      | where kind="deploymentconfig" OR kind="replicationcontroller" OR match(lower(_raw),"deploymentconfig")
      | eval dc_name=if(kind="deploymentconfig",obj,null())
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(coalesce(dc_name,obj)))
      | eval lane="evt_lane"
      | eval hot_reason=if(match(reason_ev,"DeploymentCreated|DeploymentScaled|DeploymentRolledBack|DeploymentPaused|DeploymentResumed|ProgressDeadlineExceeded|FailedRollout"),1,0)
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_evt_t latest(reason_ev) AS evt_reason latest(msg_ev) AS evt_msg max(hot_reason) AS hot_reason_flag BY cluster namespace dc_name lane ]
    [ search index=ocp_events (sourcetype="k8s_events" OR sourcetype="ocp_events") earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_namespace, involved_namespace, object_namespace, namespace, ""))))
      | eval kind=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_kind, involved_kind, object_kind, ""))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name, involved_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval msg_hp=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(message, Message, ""))))
      | where kind="pod" AND (match(msg_hp,"lifecycle|hook|pre|mid|post") OR match(lower(reason),"failed|backoff|error"))
      | rex field=msg_hp "deploymentconfig[\\s/]+(?<dc_from_msg>[a-z0-9][a-z0-9\\-]*[a-z0-9])"
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(coalesce(dc_from_msg, "")))
      | eval lane="hook_lane"
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_hook_t latest(reason) AS hook_reason latest(msg_hp) AS hook_msg BY cluster namespace dc_name lane ]
| eval ist_tmp=if(lane=="ist_lane", ist_bump_any, null())
| eventstats max(ist_tmp) AS ns_ist_bump BY cluster namespace
| where lane!="ist_lane"
| eval ist_bump_any=coalesce(ns_ist_bump, 0)
| eval namespace=lower(trim(coalesce(namespace, "unknown_namespace")))
| eval dc_name=lower(trim(coalesce(dc_name, "unknown_dc")))
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(latest_version) AS latest_version max(avail_rep) AS avail_rep max(spec_rep) AS spec_rep max(avail_flag) AS avail_flag max(prog_flag) AS prog_flag max(repfail_flag) AS repfail_flag first(progressing_reason) AS progressing_reason first(strategy_type) AS strategy_type max(stuck_version_creep) AS stuck_version_creep max(rc_distinct) AS rc_distinct max(rc_active_rows) AS rc_active_rows max(rc_ready_pos) AS rc_ready_pos max(ist_bump_any) AS ist_bump_any max(last_evt_t) AS last_evt_t first(evt_reason) AS evt_reason first(evt_msg) AS evt_msg max(hot_reason_flag) AS hot_reason_flag max(last_hook_t) AS last_hook_t first(hook_reason) AS hook_reason first(hook_msg) AS hook_msg BY cluster namespace dc_name
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace dc_name
    [ search index=ocp_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:metrics" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, Value, "")),10)
      | where match(mn,"kube_replicationcontroller_(status_ready_replicas|spec_replicas)")
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, exported_namespace, ""))))
      | eval rc_metric=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(replicationcontroller, rc, exported_replicationcontroller, ""))))
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_config, openshift_io_deployment_config_name, dc_name, ""))))
      | eval dc_name=if(len(dc_name)==0, rc_metric, dc_name)
      | stats latest(eval(if(match(mn,"spec_replicas"),mv,null()))) AS ksm_spec_rep latest(eval(if(match(mn,"ready"),mv,null()))) AS ksm_ready_rep BY cluster namespace dc_name ]
| eval rc_overlap_cnt=if(rc_distinct>1 AND rc_ready_pos>1, rc_distinct, rc_ready_pos)
| eval hook_hint=if(isnotnull(hook_msg) AND len(hook_msg)>0,1,0)
| eval rep_gap=if(isnotnull(ksm_spec_rep) AND isnotnull(ksm_ready_rep), ksm_spec_rep-ksm_ready_rep, null())
| eventstats sum(repfail_flag) AS ns_repfail_cnt BY cluster namespace
| eventstats perc90(rc_overlap_cnt) AS ns_overlap_p90 BY cluster namespace
| eval severity=case(
    match(lower(progressing_reason),"progressdeadlineexceeded") OR match(lower(evt_reason),"progressdeadlineexceeded"), "page",
    repfail_flag==1 OR match(lower(evt_reason),"failedrollout"), "page",
    avail_flag==1 AND prog_flag==1 AND stuck_version_creep==1, "page",
    hook_hint==1 AND prog_flag==1, "warn",
    rc_overlap_cnt>=2 AND coalesce(ns_overlap_p90,0)>=2 AND prog_flag==1, "warn",
    ist_bump_any==1 AND stuck_version_creep==1, "warn",
    isnotnull(rep_gap) AND rep_gap>0 AND prog_flag==1, "warn",
    match(lower(evt_reason),"deploymentpaused"), "info",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    match(lower(progressing_reason),"progressdeadlineexceeded"), "oc_describe_dc_check_timeout_interval_and_pod_readiness",
    repfail_flag==1, "oc_get_pods_and_logs_for_latest_rc_replica_failure",
    hook_hint==1, "oc_logs_hook_pod_and_validate_lifecycle_commands",
    ist_bump_any==1 AND stuck_version_creep==1, "verify_imagechange_trigger_secret_and_imagestreamtag_import",
    rc_overlap_cnt>=2 AND prog_flag==1, "oc_get_rc_compare_generations_and_review_maxsurge_maxunavailable",
    match(lower(evt_reason),"deploymentrolledback"), "correlate_oc_rollback_audit_and_validate_template",
    match(lower(evt_reason),"deploymentpaused"), "confirm_intentional_oc_rollout_pause_then_resume",
    true(), "oc_rollout_status_dc_and_review_events")
| join type=left max=0 cluster
    [| inputlookup openshift_cluster_inventory.csv
     | fields cluster, cluster_tier, owner_team, environment]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier, "unrated"),
       owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned"),
       environment=coalesce(environment, "unknown")
| table cluster namespace dc_name latest_version avail_rep spec_rep progressing_reason strategy_type stuck_version_creep rc_overlap_cnt repfail_flag ist_bump_any evt_reason hook_hint ksm_ready_rep ksm_spec_rep rep_gap ns_repfail_cnt severity recommended_action last_seen cluster_tier owner_team environment
description: Detects OpenShift DeploymentConfig rollout stalls and failures on the apps.openshift.io/v1 plane where ImageChange ConfigChange and Manual triggers materialize sequential ReplicationControllers instead of Kubernetes apps/v1 ReplicaSets. The analytic correlates status.conditions for Available Progressing and ReplicaFailure including Progressing.reason semantics, monotonic latestVersion increases against flat availableReplicas, ImageStreamTag generation churn that should advance rollouts, per-namespace events that mirror oc rollout status narratives, lifecycle hook pod completion failures on pre mid and post phases, Rolling versus Recreate versus Custom strategy parameters that govern maxSurge maxUnavailable intervalSeconds timeoutSeconds and updatePeriodSeconds, and ReplicationController overlap windows where more than one RC retains ready replicas during blue-green style cutovers or stuck scale-down. Splunk multisearch fuses structured DC exports, RC inventory, IST bumps, event reasons, optional kube-state-metrics replicationcontroller gauges, and severity tiers that explicitly differentiate this control from vanilla Deployment ReplicaSet analytics.
value: Application and platform teams regain a single timeline when legacy DeploymentConfig workloads still power critical namespaces: a silent latestVersion climb without ready replica growth strands traffic on an aging ReplicationController while hooks or triggers keep firing, and console views alone rarely preserve the cross-signal story auditors expect after an incident. A tamper-resistant ledger ties API snapshots, image tag bumps, controller events, optional metric convergence, and recommended next steps so release managers shorten mean time to innocence without conflating build failures, cluster upgrades, or generic pod crash churn. Risk reviewers receive evidence that OpenShift-specific rollout machinery remains under continuous review even as estates migrate toward Deployments.
implementation: Land ocp_deploymentconfig and ocp_replicationcontroller JSON snapshots, ocp_imagestreamtag bumps, k8s_events and ocp_audit into ocp_events and ocp_audit with consistent cluster labels; add optional prometheus lines for kube_replicationcontroller gauges into ocp_metrics; normalize DC and RC fields in props; save openshift_uc_3_3_16_deploymentconfig_rollout on a fifteen minute cadence over earliest=-6h@h; route page and warn severities to platform and application on-call; archive weekly CSV exports with exporter commit hashes for release governance.
visualization: Severity-colored table by cluster namespace dc_name with drilldowns to raw ocp_deploymentconfig JSON ocp_replicationcontroller inventory ocp_imagestreamtag rows k8s_events text and prometheus replicationcontroller metrics; timeline of latest_version against avail_rep; single-value tiles for page severity counts per fleet.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Change.action) AS change_action latest(Change.object) AS change_object latest(Change.user) AS change_user FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.dest
| rename Change.dest AS cim_dest
| join type=left max=0 cim_dest
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Application_State.state) AS app_state latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Application_State.dest
     | rename Application_State.dest AS cim_dest ]
| where like(lower(change_object), "%deploymentconfig%") OR like(lower(change_object), "%replicationcontroller%") OR like(lower(app_info), "%openshift%deployment%")
| table cim_dest change_action change_object change_user app_state app_info
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Application_State summaries for OpenShift workload entities mapped into Application_State.dest or info fields carrying DeploymentConfig condition text, and Change summaries for audit or GitOps trails that mutate apps.openshift.io DeploymentConfig or ReplicationController objects; retain thirty days for quarterly release attestations.

references.title: OpenShift Documentation — Viewing deployment configuration Kubernetes resources
Red Hat Blog — OpenShift deployment strategies for administrators

knownFalsePositives: Intentional oc rollout pause during canary or traffic-shadow experiments leaves Progressing true with deliberate partial replica states until operators oc rollout resume; join alerts to change calendars or suppress when pause_authorized metadata appears on HTTP Event Collector events. Lifecycle hook pods that run long database migrations may exceed default timeoutSeconds while still healthy; service owners should document extended windows and raise thresholds rather than treating every long hook as an outage. Rolling strategies with maxSurge greater than zero routinely create ReplicationController overlap windows where more than one RC shows ready replicas briefly; require sustained rc_overlap_cnt beyond vendor-guided durations before paging leadership. Recreate strategies may show transient zero ready replicas during tear-down phases that are expected though customer-facing monitors should still fire separately when routes serve errors. ImageStreamTag bumps in shared openshift namespace base layers can fan out many DeploymentConfigs simultaneously; fleet-level ist_bump spikes should downgrade per-application pages until cluster-wide correlation confirms a single root cause. Blue-green style cutovers that intentionally keep two RC generations warm for route testing resemble failure in naive overlap detectors; annotate dc_name rows with blue_green_expected in lookups when your pipeline relies on that pattern. Admission webhook denials on ReplicationController create can stall latestVersion without ReplicaFailure flipping immediately; corroborate ocp_audit before blaming image content. kube-state-metrics label schemes differ across chart versions; a single scrape gap can null ksm_ready_rep while API snapshots remain authoritative. Lab namespaces that continuously churn DeploymentConfigs for pipeline tests will page unless routed to non-production indexes. oc rollback during incident response produces DeploymentRolledBack events that look severe until audit_actor and bridge notes confirm intent. ConfigChange triggers firing on frequent ConfigMap edits during autoscaling sidecars can increment latestVersion without meaningful image changes; pair with diff tools before opening release defects.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode analytics on sustained DeploymentConfig rollout stalls by cluster, namespace, and dc entity

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control with the OpenShift platform engineering lead who stewards apps.openshift.io APIs, the application reliability engineers who still operate DeploymentConfig-backed services, and the observability engineers who operate Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens plus collectors that export OpenShift API state and Kubernetes events. This use case isolates DeploymentConfig objects in group apps.openshift.io version one, the legacy-but-supported OpenShift workload primitive that pairs declarative triggers with a rollout controller producing monotonically increasing status.latestVersion values and child ReplicationController objects rather than the apps/v1 Deployment and ReplicaSet pattern that UC-3.2.6 treats as primary. A DeploymentConfig encodes ImageChange triggers against ImageStreamTag objects, ConfigChange triggers against template and environment mutations, and Manual triggers for explicit oc rollout latest workflows. Each successful trigger or template revision typically creates a new ReplicationController labeled openshift.io/deployment-config.name while prior RC generations scale down according to strategy semantics. status.conditions publish Available Progressing and ReplicaFailure with reasons and messages that operators read beside oc describe deploymentconfig. Progressing.reason values such as NewReplicationControllerCreated NewReplicationControllerAvailable ProgressDeadlineExceeded and ReplicationControllerUpdated narrate whether the controller is still creating RCs, waiting for readiness, exceeded timeoutSeconds budgets, or reconciling scale. ReplicaFailure surfaces when pods owned by the active RC cannot become ready. Lifecycle hooks run dedicated hook pods for lifecycle.pre lifecycle.mid and lifecycle.post phases on Rolling and Recreate strategies; hook failures block progression before mid and post phases advance. spec.strategy.type selects Rolling Recreate or Custom; Rolling reads maxSurge maxUnavailable intervalSeconds timeoutSeconds updatePeriodSeconds from rollingParams while Recreate honors timeoutSeconds on recreateParams. Custom strategy delegates to user processes and demands tighter external monitoring because controller-native signals may be thinner.

UC-3.2.6 remains vanilla Kubernetes Deployment condition and ReplicaSet churn analytics without OpenShift DeploymentConfig triggers or ReplicationController lineage. UC-3.3.7 remains BuildConfig and Build phase failures through build.openshift.io that produce images; this UC begins after a successful push updates an ImageStreamTag that an ImageChange trigger references. UC-3.3.1 remains ClusterVersion and Cluster Version Operator upgrade progression on config.openshift.io, not per-namespace application rollout objects. UC-3.2.15 remains DaemonSet rollout drift. UC-3.3.17 remains MachineConfigPool degradation on machineconfiguration.openshift.io. Splunk becomes the cross-plane ledger that fuses periodic oc get dc exports, oc get rc lineage views, oc rollout status dc semantics, ImageStreamTag generation history, namespace Events, optional kube-state-metrics replicationcontroller replica gauges, and audit attribution for oc rollback or oc rollout cancel without pretending oc replace.

Index and permission design land before parsers. Designate index=ocp_events for sourcetype=ocp_deploymentconfig structured snapshots, sourcetype=ocp_replicationcontroller RC inventory, sourcetype=ocp_imagestreamtag tag bumps, and sourcetype=k8s_events Kubernetes Events mirrored from the API server. Designate index=ocp_audit for sourcetype=ocp_audit administrative verbs on deploymentconfigs and replicationcontrollers. Designate index=ocp_metrics when you federate kube-state-metrics or in-cluster Prometheus lines that expose kube_replicationcontroller_status_ready_replicas and kube_replicationcontroller_spec_replicas. Issue distinct HEC tokens per environment with least privilege: platform site reliability roles may search all sourcetypes; application teams receive filtered views by namespace allow lists. Retention should cover at least fourteen days hot for incident replay on stuck Progressing windows, thirty days for monthly release governance reviews, and align cold storage with enterprise logging policy. Legal review should confirm image digests and internal repository hostnames in messages are acceptable in Splunk.

RBAC and safety: exporters that run oc get deploymentconfig -o json or informers on apps.openshift.io must use dedicated ServiceAccounts with get list watch on deploymentconfigs and replicationcontrollers in scoped namespaces or cluster-wide read when policy permits, not cluster-admin credentials on employee laptops. Run collectors from hardened management hosts or continuous integration workers with short-lived kubeconfigs. When collecting ImageStreamTag objects, include imagestream.image.openshift.io read permissions without granting Secret read to pull credentials unless policy demands correlated diagnostics.

Field normalization expectations: flatten metadata.namespace into namespace, metadata.name into dc_name for DeploymentConfig rows, status.latestVersion into latest_version numeric, status.availableReplicas into avail_rep, status.replicas into status_rep, flatten conditions into available_ok progressing_ok repfail_ok with paired progressing_reason repfail_message, flatten spec.strategy.type into strategy_type, flatten rollingParams and recreateParams fields into max_surge max_unavailable interval_sec timeout_sec update_period_sec, flatten trigger summaries into trigger_hint strings for ImageChange and ConfigChange detection, and preserve cluster identity on every event as cluster openshift_cluster or cluster_name from management-cluster context. For ReplicationController rows, flatten metadata.labels openshift.io/deployment-config.name into dc_name, metadata.annotations openshift.io/deployment.phase into rc_phase when present, status.replicas into rc_spec_rep, status.readyReplicas into rc_ready_rep, and metadata.name into rc_name. For ImageStreamTag rows, flatten metadata.namespace into namespace, metadata.name into ist_name, tag generation or image metadata fields into ist_gen, and image docker reference digests into image_ref when exporters provide them. If you ingest raw JSON without KV extraction, add props.conf INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json and FIELDALIAS rules so coalesce ladders in the saved search still resolve.

Risk briefing: Progressing=True is normal during legitimate rollouts; alerts must compare dwell time, latestVersion deltas against availableReplicas stagnation, and ReplicationController overlap against strategy expectations rather than treating every Progressing flip as an incident. Rolling strategies intentionally run transient overlap windows; multiple RC rows with ready replicas above zero can be healthy during maxSurge phases or blue-green cutovers until timeoutSeconds elapses. Recreate strategies tear down old pods before new pods start; brief zero ready replicas may be expected though customer impact is higher, so pair availability monitors with route health checks. ImageStreamTag bumps from shared base-image namespaces can fan out many DeploymentConfigs in one minute; fleet noise requires cluster-level eventstats context before executive escalation. Hook pods that run database migrations may exceed default timeoutSeconds during large tenants; tune thresholds using service-level knowledge rather than generic platform defaults. oc rollout pause during canary experiments sets deliberate mid-rollout states that resemble failures until you read pause annotations or CLI state.

Differentiation recap: OpenShift DeploymentConfig triggers ReplicationController lifecycle and hook semantics on apps.openshift.io, not Kubernetes Deployment ReplicaSet conditions alone, not BuildConfig build phases, not ClusterVersion upgrades, not DaemonSet node coverage, not MachineConfigPool OS configuration convergence.

Operational posture: align telemetry with change records by stamping git_commit or change_ticket_id onto HEC events when GitOps controllers mutate DeploymentConfig objects. When Splunk shows latestVersion climbing without ImageStreamTag bumps, suspect ConfigChange triggers or manual oc rollout latest actions; correlate audit_actor fields. When Splunk shows ImageStreamTag bumps without latestVersion movement, suspect trigger disabled paused DeploymentConfig or admission denial on replicationcontrollers; pivot to ocp_audit. Capture approved oc rollback windows in a lookup keyed on cluster and namespace so future searches annotate intentional DeploymentRolledBack storms.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes Splunk multisearch can fuse: periodic DeploymentConfig API snapshots, ReplicationController inventory snapshots, ImageStreamTag generation snapshots or watch streams, Kubernetes Events for DeploymentConfig and ReplicationController involved objects, and optional kube-state-metrics replicationcontroller replica gauges from Prometheus text lines.

DeploymentConfig snapshot lane: schedule a Python or Go utility every two to five minutes from a management host with kubeconfig authentication. The utility should run oc get deploymentconfig -A -o json on estates where policy permits cluster-wide reads, or iterate namespaces from an allow list. Iterate items[], flatten status.conditions into available_ok progressing_ok repfail_ok with progressing_reason and repfail_message, flatten status.latestVersion status.availableReplicas status.replicas status.unavailableReplicas, flatten spec.strategy.type and nested rollingParams recreateParams customParams summaries, flatten spec.triggers into trigger_types and image trigger from.name from.kind namespaces, and emit sourcetype=ocp_deploymentconfig with source=oc_get_dc. Include a sha256 hash of the canonical JSON body without volatile resourceVersion when tamper-evident auditing is required.

ReplicationController snapshot lane: on the same cadence run oc get replicationcontroller -A -o json or informer watches filtered to objects carrying label openshift.io/deployment-config.name. Emit sourcetype=ocp_replicationcontroller events with cluster namespace dc_name rc_name rc_phase rc_spec_rep rc_ready_rep deployment_annotation openshift.io/deployment.version when present, and owner references if your exporter preserves them. These rows power overlap detection when more than one RC for the same dc_name shows rc_ready_rep greater than zero beyond expected surge windows.

ImageStreamTag lane: run oc get imagestreamtag -A -o json or watch imagestream.image.openshift.io/v1 ImageStreamTag objects targeted by DeploymentConfig image change triggers. Emit sourcetype=ocp_imagestreamtag with namespace ist_name ist_gen image_ref and bump_epoch. Correlate bumps to DC namespace when triggers reference local ImageStreamTag names.

Kubernetes Events lane: forward Events with sourcetype=k8s_events using Splunk OpenShift App patterns or an OpenTelemetry Collector k8s_events receiver. Preserve involvedObject.kind involvedObject.name involvedObject.namespace reason message firstTimestamp lastTimestamp count type and cluster. Retain DeploymentConfig and ReplicationController involved objects. Normalize reason case to match DeploymentCreated DeploymentScaled DeploymentRolledBack DeploymentPaused DeploymentResumed ProgressDeadlineExceeded FailedRollout and OpenShift-specific rollout messages your minor emits.

Audit lane: ship Kubernetes API audit logs into index=ocp_audit with sourcetype=ocp_audit. Ensure objectRef.apiGroup captures apps.openshift.io, objectRef.resource captures deploymentconfigs or replicationcontrollers, verbs capture patch update create delete, user.username captures actors issuing oc rollout undo oc rollback oc rollout cancel or GitOps service accounts, and responseStatus.code captures admission failures.

Prometheus lane: federate kube-state-metrics targets that expose kube_replicationcontroller_spec_replicas and kube_replicationcontroller_status_ready_replicas with labels namespace replicationcontroller or rc, and forward lines into index=ocp_metrics with sourcetype=prometheus:metrics. Preserve namespace and replicationcontroller labels for joins to dc_name via openshift.io/deployment-config.name conventions when your relabel_config copies labels.

Example oc verification operators run during design reviews:

oc get dc -n app-ns -o wide
oc describe dc app-dc -n app-ns
oc get rc -n app-ns -l openshift.io/deployment-config.name=app-dc
oc rollout status dc/app-dc -n app-ns
oc rollout history dc/app-dc -n app-ns

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE sufficiently large for JSON, TIMESTAMP_FIELDS respected when HEC time is authoritative, LINE_BREAKER rules for NDJSON watch streams, FIELDALIAS copying openshift_io_deployment_config_name into dc_name when label maps flatten differently.

Pre-save validation: index=ocp_events sourcetype=ocp_deploymentconfig earliest=-1h must return non-zero counts after the first interval; ocp_replicationcontroller ocp_imagestreamtag k8s_events arms should return rows during intentional test rollouts; ocp_audit should show verbs during controlled oc patch deploymentconfig tests; prometheus lines should include kube_replicationcontroller after scrape begins.

Collector hardening: TLS to Splunk, quarterly HEC token rotation, separate dev and prod index destinations, region and cloud_account labels on every event when one tenant spans continents.

Dashboards: Splunk OpenShift App panels should include a matrix by cluster namespace dc_name with severity coloring from this saved search, drilldowns to raw JSON for each sourcetype, and timeline overlays for latest_version against avail_rep.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as openshift_uc_3_3_16_deploymentconfig_rollout with a fifteen minute schedule, dispatch earliest=-6h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is page or warn. Throttle duplicate cluster namespace dc_name rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include recommended_action progressing_reason latest_version avail_rep and rc_overlap_cnt in pager bodies so incident commanders open the correct runbook section without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative for operators: multisearch fans DeploymentConfig snapshots, ReplicationController overlap summaries, ImageStreamTag bump rates, Kubernetes Events with rollout reasons, and optional kube-state-metrics replicationcontroller replica convergence so a silent metrics outage does not hide a genuine ProgressDeadlineExceeded story. coalesce() absorbs field naming drift between exporters. streamstats ordered by dc_name measures short-term latest_version velocity against avail_rep deltas for stuck progression hints. eventstats adds namespace-level context such as counts of DeploymentConfigs reporting ReplicaFailure and ninetieth percentile overlap scores. case() maps ProgressDeadlineExceeded ReplicaFailure sustained hook hints overlap anomalies combined with flat availability, and ImageStreamTag bump without version advance into page warn or info tiers. recommended_action encodes concise verbs referencing oc describe dc oc logs for hook pods oc get rc oc rollout resume oc rollback documentation without pretending Splunk replaces the OpenShift CLI.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.3.16 OpenShift DeploymentConfig rollout reliability — ocp_deploymentconfig ocp_replicationcontroller ocp_imagestreamtag k8s_events prometheus RC metrics. Tunables: earliest=-6h@h latest=now; stuck_prog_min=25 overlap_warn=2")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=ocp_events OR index=ocp_audit) sourcetype="ocp_deploymentconfig" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, metadata_namespace, object_namespace, ""))))
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dc_name, name, metadata_name, deploymentconfig_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval latest_version=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(latest_version, status_latestVersion, dc_latest_version, "0")),10)
      | eval avail_rep=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(avail_rep, availableReplicas, status_availableReplicas, "0")),10)
      | eval spec_rep=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(spec_rep, replicas, status_replicas, spec_replicas, "0")),10)
      | eval av_ok=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(conditions_available_status, available_status, cond_available, ""))))
      | eval pr_ok=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(conditions_progressing_status, progressing_status, cond_progressing, ""))))
      | eval rf_ok=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(conditions_replicafailure_status, replicafailure_status, cond_replicafailure, ""))))
      | eval progressing_reason=trim(toString(coalesce(conditions_progressing_reason, progressing_reason, prog_reason, "")))
      | eval strategy_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(strategy_type, spec_strategy_type, strategy, ""))))
      | eval avail_flag=if(match(av_ok,"false|0"),1,0)
      | eval prog_flag=if(match(pr_ok,"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval repfail_flag=if(match(rf_ok,"true|1"),1,0)
      | sort 0 + cluster + namespace + dc_name - _time
      | streamstats window=3 current=t global=f last(latest_version) AS prev_ver last(avail_rep) AS prev_avail BY cluster namespace dc_name
      | eval ver_delta=latest_version-coalesce(prev_ver,latest_version)
      | eval avail_delta=avail_rep-coalesce(prev_avail,avail_rep)
      | eval stuck_version_creep=if(ver_delta>0 AND avail_delta<=0 AND prog_flag==1,1,0)
      | eval lane="dc_lane"
      | fields _time cluster namespace dc_name latest_version avail_rep spec_rep avail_flag prog_flag repfail_flag progressing_reason strategy_type stuck_version_creep lane ]
    [ search index=ocp_events sourcetype="ocp_replicationcontroller" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, metadata_namespace, ""))))
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dc_name, label_openshift_io_deployment_config_name, openshift_io_deployment_config_name, deployment_config_name, ""))))
      | eval rc_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(rc_name, name, metadata_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval rc_ready=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rc_ready_rep, readyReplicas, status_readyReplicas, "0")),10)
      | eval rc_spec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rc_spec_rep, replicas, status_replicas, "0")),10)
      | eval lane="rc_lane"
      | where len(dc_name)>0
      | eval rc_active=if(rc_ready>0 OR rc_spec>0,1,0)
      | stats dc(rc_name) AS rc_distinct sum(rc_active) AS rc_active_rows sum(eval(if(rc_ready>0,1,0))) AS rc_ready_pos count AS rc_obs BY cluster namespace dc_name lane ]
    [ search index=ocp_events sourcetype="ocp_imagestreamtag" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, metadata_namespace, ""))))
      | eval ist_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(ist_name, name, metadata_name, tag_name, ""))))
      | eval ist_gen=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ist_gen, generation, tag_generation, "0")),10)
      | eval lane="ist_lane"
      | sort 0 + cluster + namespace + ist_name - _time
      | streamstats window=2 current=t global=f last(ist_gen) AS prev_igen BY cluster namespace ist_name
      | eval ist_bump=if(ist_gen>coalesce(prev_igen,ist_gen),1,0)
      | stats max(_time) AS last_ist_t max(ist_bump) AS ist_bump_any BY cluster namespace
      | eval lane="ist_lane" ]
    [ search index=ocp_events (sourcetype="k8s_events" OR sourcetype="ocp_events") earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval kind=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_kind, involved_kind, object_kind, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_namespace, involved_namespace, object_namespace, namespace, ""))))
      | eval obj=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name, involved_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval reason_ev=trim(toString(coalesce(reason, Reason, event_reason, "")))
      | eval msg_ev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(message, Message, event_message, ""))))
      | where kind="deploymentconfig" OR kind="replicationcontroller" OR match(lower(_raw),"deploymentconfig")
      | eval dc_name=if(kind="deploymentconfig",obj,null())
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(coalesce(dc_name,obj)))
      | eval lane="evt_lane"
      | eval hot_reason=if(match(reason_ev,"DeploymentCreated|DeploymentScaled|DeploymentRolledBack|DeploymentPaused|DeploymentResumed|ProgressDeadlineExceeded|FailedRollout"),1,0)
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_evt_t latest(reason_ev) AS evt_reason latest(msg_ev) AS evt_msg max(hot_reason) AS hot_reason_flag BY cluster namespace dc_name lane ]
    [ search index=ocp_events (sourcetype="k8s_events" OR sourcetype="ocp_events") earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_namespace, involved_namespace, object_namespace, namespace, ""))))
      | eval kind=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_kind, involved_kind, object_kind, ""))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name, involved_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval msg_hp=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(message, Message, ""))))
      | where kind="pod" AND (match(msg_hp,"lifecycle|hook|pre|mid|post") OR match(lower(reason),"failed|backoff|error"))
      | rex field=msg_hp "deploymentconfig[\\s/]+(?<dc_from_msg>[a-z0-9][a-z0-9\\-]*[a-z0-9])"
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(coalesce(dc_from_msg, "")))
      | eval lane="hook_lane"
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_hook_t latest(reason) AS hook_reason latest(msg_hp) AS hook_msg BY cluster namespace dc_name lane ]
| eval ist_tmp=if(lane=="ist_lane", ist_bump_any, null())
| eventstats max(ist_tmp) AS ns_ist_bump BY cluster namespace
| where lane!="ist_lane"
| eval ist_bump_any=coalesce(ns_ist_bump, 0)
| eval namespace=lower(trim(coalesce(namespace, "unknown_namespace")))
| eval dc_name=lower(trim(coalesce(dc_name, "unknown_dc")))
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(latest_version) AS latest_version max(avail_rep) AS avail_rep max(spec_rep) AS spec_rep max(avail_flag) AS avail_flag max(prog_flag) AS prog_flag max(repfail_flag) AS repfail_flag first(progressing_reason) AS progressing_reason first(strategy_type) AS strategy_type max(stuck_version_creep) AS stuck_version_creep max(rc_distinct) AS rc_distinct max(rc_active_rows) AS rc_active_rows max(rc_ready_pos) AS rc_ready_pos max(ist_bump_any) AS ist_bump_any max(last_evt_t) AS last_evt_t first(evt_reason) AS evt_reason first(evt_msg) AS evt_msg max(hot_reason_flag) AS hot_reason_flag max(last_hook_t) AS last_hook_t first(hook_reason) AS hook_reason first(hook_msg) AS hook_msg BY cluster namespace dc_name
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace dc_name
    [ search index=ocp_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:metrics" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, Value, "")),10)
      | where match(mn,"kube_replicationcontroller_(status_ready_replicas|spec_replicas)")
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, exported_namespace, ""))))
      | eval rc_metric=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(replicationcontroller, rc, exported_replicationcontroller, ""))))
      | eval dc_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_config, openshift_io_deployment_config_name, dc_name, ""))))
      | eval dc_name=if(len(dc_name)==0, rc_metric, dc_name)
      | stats latest(eval(if(match(mn,"spec_replicas"),mv,null()))) AS ksm_spec_rep latest(eval(if(match(mn,"ready"),mv,null()))) AS ksm_ready_rep BY cluster namespace dc_name ]
| eval rc_overlap_cnt=if(rc_distinct>1 AND rc_ready_pos>1, rc_distinct, rc_ready_pos)
| eval hook_hint=if(isnotnull(hook_msg) AND len(hook_msg)>0,1,0)
| eval rep_gap=if(isnotnull(ksm_spec_rep) AND isnotnull(ksm_ready_rep), ksm_spec_rep-ksm_ready_rep, null())
| eventstats sum(repfail_flag) AS ns_repfail_cnt BY cluster namespace
| eventstats perc90(rc_overlap_cnt) AS ns_overlap_p90 BY cluster namespace
| eval severity=case(
    match(lower(progressing_reason),"progressdeadlineexceeded") OR match(lower(evt_reason),"progressdeadlineexceeded"), "page",
    repfail_flag==1 OR match(lower(evt_reason),"failedrollout"), "page",
    avail_flag==1 AND prog_flag==1 AND stuck_version_creep==1, "page",
    hook_hint==1 AND prog_flag==1, "warn",
    rc_overlap_cnt>=2 AND coalesce(ns_overlap_p90,0)>=2 AND prog_flag==1, "warn",
    ist_bump_any==1 AND stuck_version_creep==1, "warn",
    isnotnull(rep_gap) AND rep_gap>0 AND prog_flag==1, "warn",
    match(lower(evt_reason),"deploymentpaused"), "info",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    match(lower(progressing_reason),"progressdeadlineexceeded"), "oc_describe_dc_check_timeout_interval_and_pod_readiness",
    repfail_flag==1, "oc_get_pods_and_logs_for_latest_rc_replica_failure",
    hook_hint==1, "oc_logs_hook_pod_and_validate_lifecycle_commands",
    ist_bump_any==1 AND stuck_version_creep==1, "verify_imagechange_trigger_secret_and_imagestreamtag_import",
    rc_overlap_cnt>=2 AND prog_flag==1, "oc_get_rc_compare_generations_and_review_maxsurge_maxunavailable",
    match(lower(evt_reason),"deploymentrolledback"), "correlate_oc_rollback_audit_and_validate_template",
    match(lower(evt_reason),"deploymentpaused"), "confirm_intentional_oc_rollout_pause_then_resume",
    true(), "oc_rollout_status_dc_and_review_events")
| join type=left max=0 cluster
    [| inputlookup openshift_cluster_inventory.csv
     | fields cluster, cluster_tier, owner_team, environment]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier, "unrated"),
       owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned"),
       environment=coalesce(environment, "unknown")
| table cluster namespace dc_name latest_version avail_rep spec_rep progressing_reason strategy_type stuck_version_creep rc_overlap_cnt repfail_flag ist_bump_any evt_reason hook_hint ksm_ready_rep ksm_spec_rep rep_gap ns_repfail_cnt severity recommended_action last_seen cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[openshift_uc_3_3_16_deploymentconfig_rollout_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -6h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = openshift-platform@example.com
action.email.subject = OCP DC rollout $result.severity$ $result.cluster$ $result.namespace$/$result.dc_name$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch openshift_uc_3_3_16_deploymentconfig_rollout | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal service levels, materialize hourly ocp_deploymentconfig summaries keyed on cluster namespace dc_name into a summary index and widen alert searches to earliest=-2h@h on the summary while retaining this full search for investigations.

For Splunk ITSI optional deployments, bind KPIs to stuck_version_creep rates and to rc_overlap_cnt maxima per namespace, attaching episode policies when two consecutive windows stay at page severity for tier-one entities.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth always starts on-cluster. Run oc get dc -n target-namespace -o wide and compare latestVersion AVAILABLE READY AVAILABLE... columns to Splunk rows for the same cluster namespace and dc_name inside the last snapshot window. When numbers disagree, first verify resourceVersion freshness and indexer clock skew before blaming Splunk parsing.

Deep dive with oc describe deploymentconfig name -n namespace to read conditions messages triggers strategy parameters and events mirrored into Splunk k8s_events. Splunk should mirror Progressing and ReplicaFailure transitions within one collection interval; if not, tighten scripted input frequency or fix token expiration on the management host.

ReplicationController lineage: oc get rc -n namespace -l openshift.io/deployment-config.name=dc-name and compare rc_ready_pos summaries to Splunk rc_overlap_cnt logic during intentional Rolling updates with maxSurge greater than zero.

ImageStreamTag correlation: oc describe istag name:tag -n namespace during a controlled build pipeline completion and confirm ocp_imagestreamtag ist_gen increments align with Splunk ist_bump_any rows for the same namespace.

Events cross-check: oc get events -n namespace --field-selector involvedObject.kind=DeploymentConfig should show reasons Splunk evt_reason captures.

Audit cross-check: replay controlled oc rollout cancel or oc rollback actions and confirm ocp_audit rows include verbs actors and object names.

Prometheus cross-check: when kube_replicationcontroller lines exist, validate ksm_ready_rep against oc get rc ready counts for the newest RC generation.

Synthetic validation: in a lab namespace, introduce a failing lifecycle post hook by referencing a command that exits non-zero under change control, ingest telemetry, and confirm openshift_uc_3_3_16_deploymentconfig_rollout surfaces warn or page with hook_hint equals one. Restore a healthy hook, watch progression, and confirm severity returns to info.

Negative test: run a clean Rolling rollout with healthy probes and generous timeoutSeconds, confirm avail_rep reaches spec_rep with Progressing false and Available true, and confirm the alert wrapper returns zero rows for page and warn filters.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — ProgressDeadlineExceeded on oc rollout status: open oc describe dc for timeoutSeconds intervalSeconds updatePeriodSeconds, inspect pod events for readiness probe failures, validate route health separately from RC ready counts, and compare surge budgets against cluster capacity.

Case 2 — ReplicaFailure true with image pull errors on new RC pods: pivot to registry and secret diagnostics while keeping this UC as the convergence gate proving availableReplicas never caught up.

Case 3 — Hook pod failures on lifecycle.pre blocking mid: read hook pod logs verify command image and service account permissions extend timeoutSeconds only after service owners approve database migration windows.

Case 4 — ImageChange trigger fired but latestVersion flat: verify trigger secret references oc secrets link ImageStreamTag import success and admission errors on ReplicationController create.

Case 5 — ConfigChange loops from hot ConfigMaps: stabilize GitOps ordering use checksum annotations to reduce thrash correlate audit actors when two controllers fight the same dc.

Case 6 — Intentional oc rollout pause during canary: annotate change records suppress paging using pause_authorized metadata on HEC events or lookup-driven downgrade when oc rollout pause is deliberate.

Case 7 — oc rollback spikes DeploymentRolledBack events: validate audit_actor correlate template git history confirm customer impact window before treating as failure versus incident response.

Case 8 — Multiple RC rows ready during blue-green style overlap: confirm strategy maxSurge and service selector cutover timing escalate only when overlap exceeds timeoutSeconds or traffic still points at old pods.

Case 9 — Shared base-image namespace rebuild fans many DC rollouts: use fleet eventstats context compare cluster-wide ist_bump rates before opening single-application defects.

Case 10 — kube-state-metrics label renames null ksm joins: fall back to ocp_replicationcontroller snapshots for replica truth and open a collector ticket to restore label mapping.

Case 11 — Custom strategy with thin controller signals: require external job success metrics and application traces because DeploymentConfig conditions may lag specialized processes.

Case 12 — Healthy info severities across tier-one namespaces: quarterly spot-check Splunk rows against oc get dc for every production cluster as a control test that tokens parsers and RBAC remain aligned.

Closing checklist: multisearch lists DC RC IST event hook arms plus join metrics arm coalesce normalizes cluster namespace dc_name streamstats flags stuck_version_creep eventstats adds ns_repfail_cnt and overlap context case maps severity closing table includes cluster namespace dc_name latest_version avail_rep spec_rep progressing_reason strategy_type stuck_version_creep rc_overlap_cnt repfail_flag ist_bump_any evt_reason hook_hint ksm_ready_rep ksm_spec_rep rep_gap ns_repfail_cnt severity recommended_action last_seen for twenty-two analyst-visible columns.
grandmaExplanation: Some of our applications still update the old OpenShift way, where each release spins a new controller generation and waits for careful steps like image swaps and small helper jobs. When that sequence gets stuck, we raise a clear signal so engineers fix the release before customers ride on a half-finished swap.
hardware: Applies to Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform 4 on bare metal, VMware, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and IBM Cloud where apps.openshift.io DeploymentConfig and ReplicationController APIs remain available; x86_64 and AArch64 application nodes are in scope when telemetry labels remain consistent.
